mnemonics.title: F-O-L Rule Rememberer
mnemonics.content: FOL = Facts (Predicates) + Objects (Constants/Variables) + Links (Quantifiers & Logic Connectives)

mnemonics.title: Inference Rule Steps
mnemonics.content: MP: If P→Q and P, get Q. MT: If P→Q and ¬Q, get ¬P. UI: ∀x P(x) gives P(c). EI: ∃x P(x) gives P(c) for new c.

mnemonics.title: Resolution Process
mnemonics.content: Resolve, Resolve, Resolve - until Empty (contradiction) shows your proof's existence!

mnemonics.title: Horn Clause Memory
mnemonics.content: Horn = H-one positive literal - think 'One' and 'Horn' (single point)

mnemonics.title: Forward vs Backward
mnemonics.content: FWD: Facts→Forward→Find solution. BWD: Begin with Goal, work Backwards to facts.

acronyms.usage: Logic system with quantifiers, predicates, and variables for knowledge representation
Storage of facts and rules about a domain in AI systems
The unique most general substitution that makes two literals identical
System that applies inference rules to derive new knowledge from KB
Formula in AND of ORs, used for applying resolution
Formula with no variables, all replaced by constants

visualTips.visualization: Draw circles/rectangles representing domain. For ∀, shade entire area (all covered). For ∃, put one dot somewhere (at least one).
Think of unification as two puzzle pieces fitting together - variables act as wildcards that get filled with constants
Draw KB and negated goal as two piles. Resolution is finding matching pieces that cancel each other until nothing remains (empty = proof!)
Forward: Facts →[Rule1]→ New Fact →[Rule2]→ Goal. Backward: Goal ←[Rule1]← Subgoal ←[Rule2]← Facts

analogies.analogy: Like an encyclopedia with cross-referenced articles - facts link to other facts through rules (like 'see also' references)
Like recipes - given ingredients (premises), you follow steps (rules) to produce a dish (conclusion)
∀ is like 'every student in class must bring ID' - applies to all. ∃ is like 'someone left their umbrella' - at least one exists.
Like solving a puzzle where variables are blank spaces waiting to be filled with matching pieces from both sides
